name: Ama

notes: Every frozen row has a terminal disposition; 29 current formulations map to frozen rows, two frozen rows are stale extras, and 55 current formulations were absent from the frozen catalog.

The asserted extractor verifies all 32 Caffè, 34 Lunch & Dinner, and 34 Brunch repeater rows before exclusions, splits the four zero-proof products from their group, and preserves 100 retained presentations as 84 formulations.
Ama publishes ingredient-rich menu descriptions but no complete allergen matrix or item-level cross-contact disclosure.
Fifty-seven formulations have fixed first-party ingredient signals; 27 remain accurately unavailable rather than receiving culinary assumptions or negative safety claims.
The Wix repeater extraction path, exact source row manifests, service-period consolidation, modifier/alcohol exclusions, category assignment, and generated persistence were reviewed and hash-tested.
Nine focused catalog/reconciliation tests pass; target-only repair preserved the SHA-256 of every non-Ama restaurant, and a second target-only run preserved Ama’s SHA-256.

sourceAttempts.outcome: The frozen domain did not resolve; it is not Ama Navy Yard’s current official site.
notes: Current first-party identity is amarestaurant.bar at 885 New Jersey Ave SE.

sourceAttempts.outcome: The current restaurant-linked Toast URL returned HTTP 403 to the audit client.
notes: The official Ama site still links this ordering URL; menu reconciliation therefore uses the complete restaurant-issued Wix pages as the highest-authority accessible evidence.

findings.summary: The frozen entry mislabeled Ama Navy Yard as a Chevy Chase restaurant and used the dead amadchevychase.com domain.
findings.resolution: Corrected the entry to amarestaurant.bar, Navy Yard, and 885 New Jersey Ave SE, Washington, DC 20003.

findings.summary: The 36-row frozen Toast-era catalog contained two standalone rows absent from the current menu and omitted 55 current formulations.
findings.resolution: Replaced it with 84 current formulations built from 100 first-party source presentations; excluded one alcoholic row and two modifier rows.

findings.summary: Seventeen frozen rows disagreed with the current first-party ingredient wording: 14 overreports, two underreports, and one mixed error.
findings.resolution: Recomputed only fixed positive signals supported by current menu wording; optional choices, dietary-label implications, and unsupported cross-contact claims remain excluded.

repairs.summary: Corrected Ama’s Navy Yard identity and replaced the stale partial catalog with the asserted 84-formulation current first-party menu and conservative allergen semantics.

notes: Aperitivo Hour publishes alcoholic drinks plus complimentary rotating stuzzichini, not separately orderable food formulations, so it is captured as identity/menu-completeness evidence but excluded from the food and nonalcoholic catalog.

The current Vitello alla Milanese row simultaneously says breaded and clarified butter while carrying GF and DF labels; without a recipe or allergen matrix, no wheat or milk claim is promoted and the row remains allergen-source unavailable.
